content.text: is the fundamental unit of work. Each agent has a role, a goal, and a backstory that together define its persona — these three fields are injected into every LLM prompt the agent makes, so they shape how it reasons and responds. Agents also carry tools, a memory setting, and an LLM configuration. A crew is a group of agents that collaborate on a shared set of tasks.

attrs.alt: CrewAI Agent anatomy: central Agent node with role, goal, backstory, llm, tools, and memory properties radiating out

content.text: and provide the three required descriptors:
parameter — CrewAI supports LangChain-compatible LLMs and its own built-in wrapper.

from crewai import Agent
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini", temperature=0.2)

researcher = Agent(
    role="Senior Research Analyst",
    goal=(
        "Uncover cutting-edge developments in AI and data science, "
        "produce clear and accurate summaries."
    ),
    backstory=(
        "You have 10 years of experience in technology research. "
        "You are meticulous, always cite your sources, and never speculate."
    ),
    llm=llm,
    verbose=True,
)
The agent's job title — included in every prompt
What the agent is trying to achieve overall
Background context that shapes the agent's reasoning style
The language model; defaults to the OPENAI_MODEL_NAME env var
List of Tool objects the agent can call
Print each thought step to stdout (useful for debugging)
Whether the agent can delegate tasks to other crew members
Enable short-term memory for context retention within a task
Maximum reasoning iterations before giving up (default 15)
Rate limit: maximum requests per minute to the LLM
Tools let an agent interact with the outside world: search the web, read files, call APIs. Pass a list of
parameter. The agent decides when to call a tool based on the task it is working on.

from crewai import Agent
from crewai_tools import SerperDevTool, FileReadTool
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# SerperDevTool needs SERPER_API_KEY env var
search_tool = SerperDevTool()
file_tool = FileReadTool()

researcher = Agent(
    role="Research Analyst",
    goal="Find accurate, up-to-date information on assigned topics.",
    backstory="You are an expert researcher who never guesses — you verify facts.",
    llm=llm,
    tools=[search_tool, file_tool],
    verbose=True,
)
package provides ready-made tools for common tasks:
Wrap any Python function as a CrewAI tool using the
. The function's name, docstring, and type hints become the tool's description that the agent uses to decide when to call it.
(the default), an agent can ask another crew member to handle a subtask it cannot complete itself. The delegating agent frames a question and the receiving agent answers it. Set
for agents that should always act independently without involving other crew members.

from crewai import Agent
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# This agent can delegate to teammates
lead_analyst = Agent(
    role="Lead Analyst",
    goal="Produce a comprehensive research report by coordinating the team.",
    backstory="You are the senior analyst who directs research and writing tasks.",
    llm=llm,
    allow_delegation=True,
)

# This agent works independently
data_scientist = Agent(
    role="Data Scientist",
    goal="Run data analysis and return precise numerical results.",
    backstory="You work with data only. You do not delegate; you compute.",
    llm=llm,
    allow_delegation=False,  # never delegate
)

on an agent to print its chain-of-thought reasoning to stdout as it works through a task. This shows each tool call, observation, and reasoning step — the standard ReAct loop. Disable it in production to reduce noise.

# With verbose=True, the console shows:
# > Entering new AgentExecutor chain...
# Thought: I need to search for the latest AI news.
# Action: search
# Action Input: {"query": "AI news 2024"}
# Observation: [search results]
# Thought: I have enough information to answer.
# Final Answer: ...

# Disable for production:
agent = Agent(
    role="...", goal="...", backstory="...",
    llm=llm,
    verbose=False,   # silent execution
)
By default, CrewAI uses the model specified by the
). You can pass any LangChain-compatible LLM directly to the agent's
parameter to use a different model or provider.

import os
from crewai import Agent
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_anthropic import ChatAnthropic

# OpenAI
gpt_agent = Agent(
    role="Writer",
    goal="Produce clear, engaging text.",
    backstory="You are an experienced technical writer.",
    llm=ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini", temperature=0.3),
)

# Anthropic Claude
claude_agent = Agent(
    role="Analyst",
    goal="Analyse data and draw accurate conclusions.",
    backstory="You specialise in data-driven analysis.",
    llm=ChatAnthropic(model="claude-3-5-haiku-20241022", temperature=0),
)

# Local Ollama model (no API key needed)
from langchain_community.chat_models import ChatOllama
local_agent = Agent(
    role="Summariser",
    goal="Produce concise summaries.",
    backstory="You specialise in distilling long documents.",
    llm=ChatOllama(model="llama3.2", temperature=0),
)

to give an agent short-term memory within a crew run. The agent retains observations from previous task steps and uses them to inform later reasoning. Combined with a crew's long-term memory store, this enables agents to build up context across multiple crew runs.

from crewai import Agent
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# Agent with short-term memory (context within the current run)
researcher = Agent(
    role="Research Analyst",
    goal="Gather and synthesise information across multiple steps.",
    backstory="You are thorough and build on your prior findings.",
    llm=llm,
    memory=True,          # retain context across task steps in this run
    verbose=True,
)
on the agent) keeps context within one crew run. Long-term memory is configured at the
level and persists across runs using a vector store. Entity memory tracks extracted facts about people, places, and concepts mentioned in tasks.

CrewAI can cache tool call results so that identical calls within a crew run return the cached result instead of re-executing. This reduces LLM costs and latency for tools that are called repeatedly with the same arguments (like repeated web searches for the same query).

from crewai import Agent
from crewai_tools import SerperDevTool

search_tool = SerperDevTool()

researcher = Agent(
    role="Research Analyst",
    goal="Research AI topics efficiently.",
    backstory="You avoid repeating the same searches twice.",
    tools=[search_tool],
    cache=True,   # cache tool results within this crew run
)
function to an agent to receive a notification after every reasoning step (tool call + observation). Use this for logging, telemetry, or streaming progress to a UI.

from crewai import Agent
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

def log_step(step_output):
    print(f"[step] tool={step_output.tool}, input={str(step_output.tool_input)[:60]}")
    print(f"       observation={str(step_output.result)[:80]}")

researcher = Agent(
    role="Researcher",
    goal="Find information efficiently.",
    backstory="You report every step of your research process.",
    llm=llm,
    step_callback=log_step,
)
CrewAI 0.80+ supports defining agents in a
file, which keeps configuration separate from code. Load it with the declarative

# config/agents.yaml
researcher:
  role: Senior Research Analyst
  goal: >
    Uncover cutting-edge developments and produce accurate summaries
    of complex technical topics.
  backstory: >
    You have 10 years of experience in technology research.
    You are meticulous and never speculate without evidence.

writer:
  role: Technical Writer
  goal: Transform research findings into clear, engaging articles.
  backstory: >
    You are an expert at making complex ideas accessible
    without sacrificing accuracy.
Vague goal: a goal like 'help with tasks' gives the agent no direction. Write a specific, measurable objective: 'Summarise the provided document in under 200 words with bullet points.'
Backstory that contradicts the goal: if the goal is to write code but the backstory says 'you never write code yourself', the agent will reason in circles. Keep role, goal, and backstory aligned.
Too many tools: an agent with 10+ tools spends reasoning tokens deciding which tool to use. Assign only the tools genuinely needed for the agent's role.

allow_delegation=True on every agent: when every agent can delegate, circular delegation loops are possible. Designate clear entry-point agents with delegation enabled and leaf-node specialists with it disabled.

You can test an agent's behaviour without assembling a full crew by calling it directly via a temporary single-task crew. This lets you verify that the agent's role, goal, and tools work as expected before combining agents.

from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

agent = Agent(
    role="Summariser",
    goal="Summarise text concisely in bullet points.",
    backstory="You specialise in distilling long documents into key points.",
    llm=llm,
    verbose=True,
)

# Wrap in a single-task, single-agent crew to execute
task = Task(
    description="Summarise the following: CrewAI is a framework for orchestrating "
                "role-playing AI agents to work together on complex tasks.",
    expected_output="Bullet points summarising the text.",
    agent=agent,
)

crew = Crew(agents=[agent], tasks=[task], verbose=True)
result = crew.kickoff()
print(result.raw)
Single-agent crews are the fastest way to validate agent configuration before building a multi-agent pipeline.
Check verbose output to confirm the agent uses tools when expected and does not hallucinate calls to non-existent tools.
Run with a low-cost model (gpt-4o-mini) during development to keep iteration fast.
An Agent in CrewAI has three required descriptors: role (job title), goal (objective), and backstory (persona context) — all injected into every LLM prompt.
Pass tools=[...] to give the agent access to external functions; use the @tool decorator to wrap any Python callable as a CrewAI tool.
allow_delegation=True (default) lets an agent hand off subtasks to other crew members; set False for agents that should always act independently.
verbose=True prints the ReAct reasoning loop (thought, action, observation) to stdout — useful for debugging, disable in production.
max_iter caps the number of reasoning iterations; max_rpm limits LLM requests per minute.
Install crewai-tools for ready-made tools: SerperDevTool (web search), FileReadTool, WebsiteSearchTool, and others.
